attractions.name: Blue Dome Entertainment District
attractions.description: The Blue Dome Entertainment District is one of Tulsa's most popular and vibrant nightlife areas, anchored by the iconic 1924 Blue Dome building. This four-block walkable neighborhood features a diverse mix of local restaurants, bars, breweries, and boutiques, making it a hub for entertainment and urban culture in downtown Tulsa (Visit Tulsa, Wikipedia).
attractions.address: 320 E 2nd St S (Historic Blue Dome Building)
attractions.hours: Open 24/7 (Individual venue hours vary; district is most active Thu-Sat, 7:00 PM - 12:00 AM) (Drive The Nation)
attractions.admission: Free to enter the district; individual business and event prices vary (Drive The Nation, MetroFamily Magazine)
highlights: The Historic 1924 Blue Dome Building (originally a Gulf Oil gas station)
Vibrant nightlife with numerous bars, pubs, and live music venues
Annual Blue Dome Arts Festival featuring local artists and musicians
Diverse dining options ranging from local diners to upscale eateries
Proximity to Historic Route 66 and urban murals
Walking distance to major downtown sports and concert venues
attractions.tips: Metered street parking is available throughout the district and is often free after 5:00 PM or 6:00 PM on weekdays and all day on Sundays. For a quieter experience, visit on weekday evenings, or come during the third weekend in May for the popular arts festival (Drive The Nation).
attractions.nearby: ONEOK Field, BOK Center, Tulsa Arts District, Greenwood Historical District, Arvest Convention Center (Wikipedia)

attractions.name: BOK Center (Bank of Oklahoma Center)
attractions.description: The BOK Center is a premier 19,199-seat multi-purpose arena located in the heart of downtown Tulsa, Oklahoma. Designed by famed architect Cesar Pelli, it serves as a primary venue for major concerts, sporting events, and family shows, and is the home of the ECHL's Tulsa Oilers hockey team and the IFL's Tulsa Oilers football team.
attractions.hours: Box Office: Tuesday - Friday (11:00 AM – 3:00 AM). On weekday event days, the Box Office opens at 11:00 AM. On weekend event days, it opens 2 hours prior to door time. Doors typically open 60 minutes before start time. BOK Center FAQ
attractions.days_open: Open for scheduled events. Box Office is open Tuesday - Friday.
attractions.admission: Prices vary by event. Tickets can be purchased via Ticketmaster or at the BOK Center Box Office. Ticketmaster, Official Box Office Info
highlights: Designed by world-renowned architect Cesar Pelli with themes of Native American, art deco, and contemporary styles. Wikipedia
Ranked 20th in the U.S. and 29th worldwide in Billboard's 2023 Boxscore Top Venue report. TulsaGo
Named 'Arena of the Year' by the International Entertainment Buyers Association (IEBA) in 2016 and 2018. BOK Center About
Has hosted major music icons including Paul McCartney, Garth Brooks (7 sold-out shows), and Metallica (setting an attendance record). BOK Center About
Serves as a recurring host for high-profile sporting events such as NCAA Basketball Tournament rounds and Big 12 Wrestling Championships. BOK Center About
attractions.tips: BOK Center is a cashless venue; only debit/credit cards or vouchers are accepted for food and merchandise. Arrive early to allow time for security and walk-through metal detectors. Follow the clear bag policy for entry. BOK Center FAQ
attractions.nearby: Arvest Convention Center, Tulsa Performing Arts Center, Woody Guthrie Center, Cain's Ballroom, Tulsa Theater, Arena District restaurants (Arena Grill, Kai Vietnamese Cuisine). Visit Tulsa, Tripadvisor

attractions.name: Boston Avenue United Methodist Church
attractions.description: Boston Avenue United Methodist Church is a National Historic Landmark in downtown Tulsa, recognized as one of the preeminent examples of ecclesiastical Art Deco architecture in the United States. Completed in 1929, the building features a soaring 225-foot tower and intricate terra cotta sculptures that serve as a "sermon in stone" for its progressive congregation (Wikipedia, Boston Avenue UMC).
attractions.hours: Monday – Friday: 8:30 a.m. – 5:00 p.m.; Sunday: 8:30 a.m. – 1:00 p.m. (Sunday Worship at 9:00 a.m. and 11:00 a.m.; Guided Tours at 12:15 p.m.) (Boston Avenue UMC)
attractions.admission: Free (Boston Avenue UMC)
highlights: Ecclesiastical Art Deco Design: A masterpiece designed by Adah Robinson and Bruce Goff, featuring a 225-foot tower topped with praying hands (Wikipedia).
The Sanctuary: A stunning circular space with a 40-foot dome that symbolizes the infinite, designed to lead all lines toward the pulpit (Boston Avenue UMC).
Artistic Details: Intricate terra cotta sculptures, stained glass with downward-flowing lines, and equestrian "Circuit Rider" statues (Boston Avenue UMC).
Foley-Baker Pipe Organ: A majestic instrument rebuilt in 2019, incorporating pipes from the original 1961/87 Möller organ (Boston Avenue UMC).
Boston Avenue Park: A serene one-acre campus addition featuring an interfaith statue representing different world religions (Boston Avenue UMC).
National Historic Landmark: Recognized by the U.S. Department of the Interior and listed on the National Register of Historic Places (National Park Service).
attractions.tips: Visit on a Sunday at 12:15 p.m. for a free guided tour starting in the second-floor library, or pick up a self-guided tour brochure during weekday office hours. While the building is an architectural marvel, it remains an active place of worship, so visitors are encouraged to be mindful of ongoing services (Boston Avenue UMC).
attractions.nearby: BOK Center, Woody Guthrie Center, Philbrook Museum of Art, Center of the Universe (Visit Tulsa)

attractions.name: James E. McNellie's Public House McNellie's official site
attractions.description: James E. McNellie's Public House is a classic Irish-style pub in downtown Tulsa that opened in 2004, inspired by the community-focused pubs of Dublin. It serves a blend of traditional Irish and modern American pub fare alongside an extensive selection of over 350 beers. McNellie's official site, Blue Dome District
attractions.address: 409 E 1st St, Tulsa, OK 74120 McNellie's official site
attractions.region: Blue Dome District, Downtown Tulsa TulsaGo, Blue Dome District
attractions.hours: Sun-Tues: 11am - 10pm; Wed-Thurs: 11am - 11pm; Fri-Sat: 11am - 12am McNellie's official site
attractions.days_open: Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday McNellie's official site
attractions.admission: Free Tripadvisor
highlights: Extensive beer selection with over 350 draft and bottled options Blue Dome District
Popular weekly specials like $3 Burger Night on Wednesdays Foursquare
Traditional Irish atmosphere inspired by authentic Dublin pubs McNellie's official site
Hosts the annual McNellie's Pub Run, a downtown Tulsa tradition RunSignup
Offers a variety of pub favorites like Fish & Chips, Cottage Pie, and the Charburger Yelp
attractions.tips: Visit on Wednesdays for the famous Burger Night specials, but be prepared for long waits due to its popularity. Street parking is limited, so consider using the nearby pay lots. Foursquare, Tripadvisor
attractions.nearby: ONEOK Field, Guthrie Green, BOK Center, and other Blue Dome District attractions like Dilly Diner and Fassler Hall Downtown Tulsa Partnership, Visit Tulsa

attractions.name: Oklahoma Jazz Hall of Fame (Jazz Depot)
attractions.description: The Oklahoma Jazz Hall of Fame is a non-profit organization dedicated to honoring the rich heritage of jazz, blues, and gospel musicians in Oklahoma. Housed in the historic Tulsa Union Depot, it operates as both a museum for music memorabilia and a premier venue for live performances.
attractions.hours: Monday, Thursday-Saturday: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Tuesday-Wednesday: 9:00 AM - 10:00 PM; Sunday: 3:00 PM - 7:00 PM (Standard hours; currently closed for renovation)
attractions.admission: Museum: Free; Live Performances: Typically $25 and under
highlights: Housed in the historic Art Deco Tulsa Union Depot building
Honors over 100 inductees including Chet Baker and Charlie Christian
Extensive collection of musical artifacts, photographs, and memorabilia
Hosts regular live jazz concerts and Tuesday night jam sessions
Educational programs focused on Oklahoma's unique musical history
Located in the historic Greenwood District area
attractions.tips: As of 2026, the facility is undergoing significant renovations and is largely closed to the public; check the official website or social media for the latest updates on special events or reopening dates. Tuesday nights are traditionally known for free jam sessions when the venue is operational.
attractions.nearby: BOK Center, Center of the Universe, ONEOK Field, Guthrie Green, Greenwood Cultural Center, Philbrook Museum of Art (Downtown)

attractions.name: ONEOK Field (Home of the Tulsa Drillers) MiLB.com
attractions.description: ONEOK Field is a premier 7,833-capacity baseball park located in the historic Greenwood District of downtown Tulsa. Opened in 2010, it serves as the home field for the Tulsa Drillers (Double-A affiliate of the LA Dodgers) and FC Tulsa soccer, offering fans modern amenities and stunning skyline views. The stadium is designed for a versatile fan experience, featuring family-friendly play zones and upscale club seating options MiLB.com, The Vista Tulsa
attractions.address: 201 N. Elgin Ave., Tulsa, OK 74120 MiLB.com
attractions.region: Historic Greenwood District / Downtown Tulsa MiLB.com
attractions.hours: Box Office: Mon-Fri 8:30 AM – 5:00 PM (Non-game days), Game days: 8:30 AM – End of game; Sat: Opens 12:00 PM; Sun: Opens 10:00 AM (Day games) or 2:00 PM (Night games). Team Store: Mon-Fri 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM MiLB.com
attractions.days_open: Monday–Friday (Offices/Store); Daily during home game stretches MiLB.com
attractions.admission: Tickets typically range from $17 to $25+ for standard seating; Kids 3 and under are Free if they do not occupy a seat SeatGeek, MiLB.com
highlights: Home of the Tulsa Drillers and FC Tulsa MiLB.com
Located in the Historic Greenwood District MiLB.com
Features the Busch Scoreboard Bar and Cadillac Stadium Club MiLB.com
Family amenities like the Red Diamond Splash Zone and Hornsby’s Hangout MiLB.com
Cashless facility with on-site reverse ATMs MiLB.com
Panoramic views of the Tulsa skyline MiLB.com
attractions.tips: Arrive at least 60 minutes early for gate openings and giveaways; the stadium is entirely cashless, so bring a credit/debit card or use the 'Reverse ATMs' on-site to exchange cash for a debit card MiLB.com, MiLB.com
attractions.nearby: Greenwood Rising Museum, Bob Dylan Center, Woody Guthrie Center, Guthrie Green, Cain’s Ballroom The Vista Tulsa

attractions.name: Philtower Building, Official Website, Wikipedia
attractions.category: Landmark, Historic Building, Skyscraper, Official Website, Wikipedia
attractions.description: The Philtower Building is an iconic 24-story skyscraper completed in 1928, designed by Edward Buehler Delk and financed by oilman Waite Phillips. It is a renowned example of Neo-Gothic and Art Deco architecture, famously topped with a 40-foot illuminated, sloping tiled roof and a copper lantern. Official Website, Wikipedia
attractions.region: Deco District, Downtown Tulsa, Official Website, Decopolis
attractions.hours: Monday - Friday: 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM (Lobby); Exterior viewable 24/7, Tripadvisor, Airial Travel
attractions.admission: Free for lobby access and exterior viewing, Tripadvisor, Airial Travel
highlights: Neo-Gothic and Art Deco architectural design by Edward Buehler Delk, Wikipedia
Striking 40-foot tall illuminated pyramidal roof covered in 16,000 colorful tiles, Decopolis
Historic 80-foot secret underground tunnel connecting to the Philcade Building, Decopolis
Ornate lobby featuring exquisite painted ceilings, brass elevator doors, and travertine marble, Tulsa Architecture
Listed on the National Register of Historic Places since 1979, Official Website
First mixed-use high-rise in Tulsa with luxury lofts on the upper floors, Official Website
attractions.tips: Visit during standard business hours to admire the lobby's ornate ceilings and brass details. Consider a guided walking tour for access to restricted areas like the historic tunnels. Tripadvisor, Atlas Obscura
attractions.nearby: Philcade Building, Atlas Life Building, Tulsa Club, Tulsa Performing Arts Center, Decopolis Art Deco Museum, Official Website, Decopolis

attractions.name: The Mayo Hotel & Residences
attractions.description: The Mayo Hotel is a historic 19-story icon in downtown Tulsa's Deco District, originally opened in 1925 as the tallest building in Oklahoma. After a $42 million renovation, it now serves as a luxury boutique hotel and residence, blending Art Deco grandeur with modern amenities. It is listed on the National Register of Historic Places and has hosted notable guests from Elvis Presley to President John F. Kennedy. Official Website
attractions.hours: Hotel: Open 24/7 (Check-in: 4:00 PM, Check-out: 12:00 PM); 1925 Bar & Lounge: Daily from 4:00 PM; 1925 Dining: Tue–Sat 5:00 PM–10:00 PM; The Mark Cafe: Mon–Fri 6:00 AM–6:00 PM, Sat 7:00 AM–6:00 PM, Sun 7:00 AM–1:00 PM. Official Website, The Mark Cafe
attractions.admission: Hotel admission is free for guests and visitors; rooms and dining vary by price. Expedia
highlights: Listed on the National Register of Historic Places Wikipedia
The Penthouse Rooftop Lounge with 360-degree views of Downtown Tulsa Official Website
Historic Crystal Ballroom and Grand Hall event spaces Official Website
On-site Mayo Museum showcasing hotel history Travel Weekly
Art Deco architecture designed by George Winkler Flickr
Luxurious suites including the Celebrity Suite with a whirlpool tub The Hotel Guru
attractions.tips: Visit the Penthouse Rooftop Lounge for sunset views and live music on Friday nights. Valet parking is available for a surcharge and is recommended during major events at the nearby BOK Center. Complimentary airport transfers and shuttle rides within 4 miles are available for hotel guests. Official Website, The Hotel Guru
attractions.nearby: BOK Center, Tulsa Performing Arts Center, Guthrie Green, Decopolis Tulsa Art Deco Museum, and Arvest Convention Center. Tripadvisor

attractions.name: Tribune Lofts
attractions.description: Tribune Lofts is a historic residential community located in the heart of the Tulsa Arts District in Downtown Tulsa (Tribune Lofts). Housed in a carefully preserved 1924 structure, the lofts feature industrial aesthetics such as concrete floors, exposed brick, and high ceilings (Zumper). Residents enjoy high-end amenities including a rooftop lounge with skyline views, a 24-hour fitness center, and a swimming pool (Tribune Lofts).
attractions.hours: Mon–Thu: 9 AM–6 PM; Fri: 9 AM–5 PM; Sat: 10 AM–2 PM; Sun: Closed (Tribune Lofts)
attractions.admission: Free (Tribune Lofts)
highlights: Historic 1924 building with unique industrial floor plans (Zumper)
Rooftop lounge offering views of the Downtown Tulsa skyline and fireworks (Tribune Lofts)
Outdoor swimming pool with tanning ledges and a fire pit (Tribune Lofts)
24-hour state-of-the-art fitness center (Tribune Lofts)
Gated community with an outdoor kitchen and grilling station (Tribune Lofts)
Prime location in the heart of the Tulsa Arts District (The Tulsa Arts District)
attractions.tips: Schedule a tour in advance to view the historic lofts and community amenities (Tribune Lofts). Visit the rooftop lounge on clear nights for the best views of the city skyline or to catch firework shows from the nearby ONEOK Field (Zumper).
attractions.nearby: ONEOK Field, BOK Center, Cain's Ballroom, Guthrie Green, Center of the Universe, and various art galleries (Zumper)

attractions.name: Tulsa Arts District
attractions.description: The Tulsa Arts District is a diverse, culturally robust neighborhood in northern downtown Tulsa, grown from a historic oil-rich past. It features an eclectic collection of bars, restaurants, galleries, museums, and historic music venues, embodying the motto 'Arrive Early, Stay Late.' (Tulsa Arts District)
attractions.hours: District: 24/7; Museums (e.g., Bob Dylan/Woody Guthrie Centers): Wed-Sun 10 AM - 6 PM (Bob Dylan Center). Bars and restaurants often stay open late daily (Tulsa Arts District).
attractions.days_open: Daily (Venues vary; many Wed-Sun) (Bob Dylan Center)
attractions.admission: Free for district exploration; Museums: Adults $15, Dual Center $22, Seniors/Veterans/Students $12, Youth/Teachers Free (Bob Dylan Center). First Friday Art Crawl offers free or reduced admission to many venues (Tulsa Arts District).
highlights: Woody Guthrie Center (Woody Guthrie Center)
Bob Dylan Center (Bob Dylan Center)
Cain's Ballroom (Tulsa Arts District)
Guthrie Green community park (Guthrie Green)
First Friday Art Crawl (Tulsa Arts District)
Tulsa Theater (Tulsa Arts District)
attractions.tips: Visit during the First Friday Art Crawl for free gallery access and a vibrant street atmosphere (Tulsa Arts District). Use the North Parking Garage at 15 South Main Street for secure parking (Tulsa Arts District). Many restaurants offer patios perfect for a date night start (Tulsa Arts District).
attractions.nearby: Blue Dome District, Greenwood Historical District, BOK Center, ONEOK Field (Tulsa Arts District)

attractions.name: Tulsa Arts District (formerly Brady Arts District)
attractions.description: The Tulsa Arts District is a diverse, culturally robust neighborhood in Downtown Tulsa that has evolved from a historic oil-rich past into a creative, community-driven hub. Home to galleries, museums, parks, and historic music venues, it serves as a central engine for the city's arts and entertainment scene. Visitors are encouraged to follow the district's motto: "Arrive Early. Stay Late." The Tulsa Arts District
attractions.address: 1 E Mathew B Brady St (District Center)
attractions.hours: District open 24/7; typical business hours 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM (varies by venue) The Tulsa Arts District
attractions.admission: Free (District access); individual venue prices vary (e.g., Woody Guthrie Center: $12 adults, Free 17 & under) Woody Guthrie Center, Bob Dylan Center
highlights: First Friday Art Crawl (monthly gallery openings and events)
Historic music venues like Cain's Ballroom and The Tulsa Theater
Guthrie Green, a vibrant public park and outdoor performance space
The Woody Guthrie Center and Bob Dylan Center museums
Eclectic mix of locally-owned restaurants, bars, and retail shops
Proximity to ONEOK Field for baseball and community events
attractions.tips: Visit on the first Friday of each month for the signature Art Crawl event. Street parking is free after 5:00 PM on weekdays and all day on weekends. Use the ParkMobile app for convenient paid parking during enforcement hours. Tulsa Arts District Parking
attractions.nearby: BOK Center, Blue Dome District, Greenwood Cultural Center, ONEOK Field Wikipedia

attractions.name: Tulsa Deco District
attractions.description: The Tulsa Deco District is a historic area in downtown Tulsa featuring one of the nation's largest concentrations of Art Deco architecture from the 1920s and 30s. This district showcases the city's immense wealth during the oil boom through iconic Zigzag and Streamline Moderne skyscrapers and lobbies. Visit Tulsa, TulsaGo
attractions.address: Centered around 5th Street and Boston Avenue, Tulsa, OK 74103
attractions.hours: District: Always open; Building lobbies: Usually 7:00 AM - 9:00 PM Monday-Saturday, 8:00 AM - 1:00 PM Sunday Decopolis
attractions.admission: Free (Walking tours range from $20 to $52) Tulsa Tours, AAA
highlights: Philcade Building
Philtower Building
Atlas Life Building
Tulsa Club Hotel
Mayo Hotel
Boston Avenue United Methodist Church
attractions.tips: Street parking is free on weekends; visit during weekday business hours to access building lobbies; stop by Decopolis on Route 66 for a free walking tour map. Tulsa Tours, Decopolis
attractions.nearby: Center of the Universe, BOK Center, Guthrie Green, Blue Dome District, Oklahoma Jazz Hall of Fame Tulsa Tours, TulsaGo

attractions.name: Tulsa Performing Arts Center (Tulsa PAC)
attractions.description: The Tulsa Performing Arts Center is a premier multi-venue arts complex located in the heart of downtown Tulsa. Designed by Minoru Yamasaki, the architect of the original World Trade Center, it serves as the primary home for the city's major performing arts organizations and Broadway tours. The facility houses four main theaters, a studio space, and a permanent collection of 76 works of art (Tulsa Performing Arts Center, Wikipedia).
attractions.hours: Administrative Office: Mon-Fri 9:00 AM - 12:00 PM, 1:00 PM - 5:00 PM; Ticket Office: Mon-Fri 10:30 AM - 5:30 PM (opens 2 hours prior to showtime on event days) (Tulsa Performing Arts Center).
attractions.days_open: Monday - Friday (Business/Ticket Office); Daily for scheduled performances (Tulsa Performing Arts Center).
attractions.admission: Ticket prices vary by performance; a $5.00 facility fee is added to all tickets. Reduced prices for students and children may be available depending on the presenter (Tulsa Performing Arts Center).
highlights: Features the 2,365-seat Chapman Music Hall, the largest of its five performance spaces.
Designed by world-renowned architect Minoru Yamasaki.
Home to Resident Partners including Tulsa Ballet, Tulsa Opera, and Tulsa Symphony.
Houses a permanent art collection consisting of 76 significant works.
Offers diverse community programs such as 'Any Given Child' and 'Broadway Senior'.
Includes flexible performance areas like the John H. Williams and Liddy Doenges theaters.
attractions.tips: The main ticket office is located in the 3rd Street Lobby. Photography and recording are strictly prohibited during performances. Visitors are encouraged to prepay for parking online to ensure availability (Tulsa Performing Arts Center).
attractions.nearby: Hyatt Regency Tulsa Downtown, The Avery Kitchen & Bar, Lotus Spa, Oklahoma World Stage (Tulsa Performing Arts Center).

attractions.name: Tulsa Theater
attractions.description: The Tulsa Theater is a historic performance venue completed in 1914, originally designed as a municipal auditorium and convention hall. Listed on the National Register of Historic Places, it is a cornerstone of the local arts scene and has hosted some of the most iconic names in show business. (Tulsa Theater Venue Info, Wikipedia)
attractions.hours: Box Office: Monday - Friday, 10:00 AM - 6:00 PM (remains open through the main act on event days). Venue: Typically opens 1 hour prior to event start time. (Tulsa Theater FAQ)
attractions.days_open: Monday - Friday (Box Office); Event days vary. (Tulsa Theater FAQ)
attractions.admission: Varies by performance; check the official website or Etix for specific show pricing. (Tulsa Theater FAQ)
highlights: Historic venue completed in 1914 and listed on the National Register of Historic Places. (Wikipedia)
Renowned for its impeccable acoustics and layout that ensures a great view from every seat. (Tulsa Theater Venue Info)
Located in the heart of the vibrant Tulsa Arts District. (The Tulsa Arts District)
Has hosted legendary performers such as Don Henley, Louis Armstrong, and Buddy Guy. (Tulsa Theater Venue Info)
Affectionately known as 'The Old Lady on Brady.' (Wikipedia)
attractions.tips: Ample free parking is available within short walking distances, while limited paid parking right beside the venue is typically $10+. No outside food or drinks are permitted, and re-entry is not allowed once your ticket is scanned. (Tulsa Theater FAQ)
attractions.nearby: Cain's Ballroom, Guthrie Green, ONEOK Field, and the Tulsa Performing Arts Center. (The Tulsa Arts District, Visit Tulsa)

attractions.name: Brookside Tulsa District
attractions.description: Brookside is a historic and vibrant entertainment district in Midtown Tulsa known as the "Restless Ribbon" for its lively atmosphere. It features a diverse mix of upscale boutiques, trendy restaurants, and professional services along South Peoria Avenue. The neighborhood offers a walkable experience that seamlessly blends a charming residential core with a high-energy social scene.
attractions.address: S Peoria Ave (between 31st St and 51st St), Tulsa, OK 74105
attractions.hours: Open 24 hours (Individual business hours typically vary from 8:00 AM – 2:00 AM)
attractions.admission: Free to visit the district; individual business prices vary.
highlights: Historic Brook Theater neon sign and marquee
Diverse dining landscape with over 35 restaurants and nightspots
Access to River Parks trails and the Gathering Place park
Boutique shopping ranging from designer clothing to Tulsa-themed gifts
Home to the Tulsa Ballet and the Discovery Lab science museum
Annual community events like the Brookside BooHaHa Halloween parade
attractions.tips: The district is highly walkable, so park once and explore the 'Restless Ribbon' on foot. Many restaurants offer patio seating, making it a great destination for al fresco dining in the spring and fall. For the best experience, visit on a weekend for brunch or during one of the district's frequent street festivals.
attractions.nearby: Gathering Place, Philbrook Museum of Art, River Parks, Zink Lake, Utica Square

attractions.name: River Parks (Riverside Park)
attractions.description: River Parks is a sprawling multi-use recreational area featuring over 26 miles of asphalt-paved trails along the scenic banks of the Arkansas River. The park system includes beautifully manicured landscapes, interactive water features, and unique life-sized bronze wildlife sculptures that highlight the local environment. It serves as a central hub for outdoor activity in Tulsa, connecting various neighborhoods and major attractions like the Gathering Place.
attractions.hours: Monday: 5:00 AM - 11:00 PM, Tuesday: 5:00 AM - 11:00 PM, Wednesday: 5:00 AM - 11:00 PM, Thursday: 5:00 AM - 11:00 PM, Friday: 5:00 AM - 11:00 PM, Saturday: 5:00 AM - 11:00 PM, Sunday: 5:00 AM - 11:00 PM
attractions.admission: Free
highlights: Over 26 miles of paved trails for cycling, running, and walking
Interactive water features and splash pads at the 41st Street Plaza
Extensive collection of life-sized bronze wildlife sculptures
18-hole and 9-hole disc golf courses located along the river banks
Scenic overlooks offering panoramic views of the Tulsa skyline and river sunsets
Direct connections to major local landmarks like the Gathering Place and Turkey Mountain
attractions.tips: The park features strictly separated lanes for pedestrians and cyclists, so be sure to follow the ground markings for safety. It is particularly popular at sunset, providing some of the best golden hour views in the city. Since the trail system is extensive, bringing a reusable water bottle is recommended, though drinking fountains are available at major plazas.
attractions.nearby: Gathering Place, Philbrook Museum of Art, Turkey Mountain Urban Wilderness Area, Blue Rose Cafe, Zink Lake

attractions.name: Cherry Street District
attractions.description: Cherry Street is a vibrant, walkable historic district in Midtown Tulsa known as a premier destination for foodies and shoppers. The area features over 20 locally-owned restaurants, unique antique boutiques, and charming 1920s architecture. It is a popular spot for brunch, late-night dining, and community events like the seasonal farmers market.
attractions.address: E. 15th St. between S. Peoria Ave. and S. Utica Ave.
attractions.hours: District: Open 24/7; Typical Shops: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM; Restaurants/Bars: 11:00 AM – 11:00 PM+
attractions.admission: Free (Public District)
highlights: Over 20 diverse locally-owned restaurants and bars
Unique antique, collectible, and clothing boutiques
Cherry Street Farmers Market on Saturday mornings (April–October)
Pedestrian-friendly atmosphere with historic 1920s and 30s architecture
Vibrant nightlife including rooftop bars and Irish pubs
Proximity to historic residential neighborhoods like Swan Lake
attractions.tips: Parking on 15th Street can be tight during peak hours, so look for public lots or side street parking. Visit on Saturday mornings during the spring and summer to experience the popular farmers market. The district is very walkable, making it ideal for exploring on foot.
attractions.nearby: Swan Lake Historic District, Philbrook Museum of Art, Utica Square, The Gathering Place, Downtown Tulsa

attractions.name: Expo Square
attractions.description: Expo Square is a premier multi-purpose event complex in Tulsa, recognized as the home of the annual Tulsa State Fair and the iconic 76-foot-tall Golden Driller statue. The 15-acre site features world-class facilities including the SageNet Center—one of the largest clearspan buildings in the world—and the historic Art Deco-style Pavilion, hosting hundreds of diverse events year-round from livestock shows and trade expos to professional sports. Wikipedia, Expo Square History
attractions.hours: Administration Office: Monday - Thursday: 9 AM - 12 PM & 1 PM - 4 PM; Friday: 9 AM - 3 PM; Saturday - Sunday: Closed. Event hours vary significantly by show. Expo Square Hours
attractions.admission: Varies by event. Admission for many trade shows and expos ranges from $5.00 to $12.00, while others are free to the public. The annual Tulsa State Fair has its own tiered pricing for gate admission and ride sheets; children 12 and under often receive free admission for specific shows when accompanied by a paid adult. Expo Square Tickets, Tickets Tulsa
highlights: The Golden Driller, an iconic 76-foot-tall statue representing Tulsa's oil heritage.
The SageNet Center, providing over 350,000 square feet of column-free event space.
The Tulsa State Fair, a major 11-day annual event held every autumn.
The Pavilion, a historic 1930s Art Deco-style arena hosting concerts and sporting events.
Year-round calendar of trade shows, including the world's largest gun show.
On-site RV Park with full hookups for traveling visitors.
attractions.tips: Always check the official online calendar before your visit as events change weekly. Wear comfortable walking shoes, as the complex is very large and requires significant walking between venues. Be sure to stop for a photo with the Golden Driller, located directly in front of the SageNet Center. Expo Square Visiting

attractions.name: Gathering Place: Tulsa's Riverfront Park gatheringplace.org

attractions.name: Oral Roberts University
attractions.description: Oral Roberts University is a private Evangelical university founded in 1963 by evangelist Oral Roberts. The campus is renowned for its distinctive futuristic architecture and is committed to providing a Spirit-empowered Christian education that develops the mind, body, and spirit.
attractions.hours: Campus Visits: Mon-Fri 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Prayer Tower Visitor Center: Mon-Sat 12:00 PM - 5:00 PM
attractions.admission: Free for campus and Prayer Tower visitor center
highlights: Prayer Tower: A 200-foot glass and steel structure with an observation deck at the heart of the campus.
Praying Hands Statue: A massive 60-foot, 30-ton bronze sculpture at the main entrance.
Avenue of Flags: A ceremonial entrance featuring flags from over 60 nations representing the student body.
Christ's Chapel: A unique 3,500-seat building designed to resemble a tent, echoing the founder's early revivals.
Mabee Center: An 8,500-seat arena hosting athletic events, concerts, and university services.
Learning Resource Center: A massive 900,000 square foot facility styled after King Solomon's Temple.
attractions.tips: Visit the Prayer Tower observation deck for a 360-degree view of the campus and Tulsa skyline. Visitors should be mindful of the university's religious environment and honor code while exploring the grounds. Check for specific tour times if you wish to see academic facilities.

attractions.name: Black Mesa State Park & Nature Preserve
attractions.description: Black Mesa State Park and Nature Preserve is home to the highest point in Oklahoma at 4,973 feet above sea level. This unique landscape features a basalt lava flow from three million years ago and is a prime destination for hiking, stargazing, and viewing preserved dinosaur tracks. It has been designated an International Dark Sky Park, offering some of the clearest nighttime viewing in the country.
attractions.hours: State Park: Open 24 hours daily; Nature Preserve: Open daily from dawn to dusk.
attractions.admission: Free to visit the Nature Preserve and hike to the summit; $10 daily parking fee for vehicles at Black Mesa State Park.
highlights: Highest point in Oklahoma (4,973 ft) at the summit monument
International Dark Sky Park designation for world-class stargazing
Preserved Jurassic-period dinosaur tracks in sandstone near Carrizo Creek
Unique volcanic basalt lava flow geography and rock formations
Home to diverse wildlife including bighorn sheep, golden eagles, and pronghorn antelope
Annual host of the Okie-Tex Star Party for amateur astronomers
attractions.tips: Start hikes at dawn during summer months to avoid extreme heat, bring at least one gallon of water per person, and wear sturdy boots for the rocky terrain. Be aware that while officially in the Central Time Zone, the local community often operates on Mountain Time.
attractions.nearby: The town of Kenton (historic ranching outpost), Dinosaur Quarry (replica dinosaur femur site), and the Tri-State Marker where Oklahoma, Colorado, and New Mexico meet.

attractions.description: Chandler Park is a 192-acre natural haven situated on the western bank of the Arkansas River, offering panoramic views of downtown Tulsa and Sand Springs. Originally donated as a 100-acre quarry in 1958, it has evolved into a premier destination for recreation with wooded areas, unique rock formations, and state-of-the-art facilities Tulsa County Parks.
attractions.hours: Park: 7:00 a.m. - 8:30 p.m. daily; Community Center: See seasonal activities; Splash Park (Seasonal): Tue-Sun 9 a.m. - 6:30 p.m. (Sessions vary) Tulsa County Parks
attractions.days_open: Park: Daily; Splash Park: Tuesday - Sunday (Closed Monday) Tulsa County Parks
attractions.admission: General Park Entry: Free; Splash Park: $5.00 per person per session (effective February 2, 2026); Private Splash Park Reservation: $150 per session (max 75 guests); Aquatics Memberships: $40 (Individual), $70 (Family) Tulsa County Parks
highlights: Large state-of-the-art Splash Park with slides and water cannons
Two 18-hole disc golf courses (Moose and Bear)
Five hiking and nature trails, including the paved Lower Level Trail
Unique rock formations popular for rock climbing and rappelling
Panoramic views of the Tulsa skyline and Arkansas River
Community center with basketball courts and recreational programs
attractions.tips: Wear sturdy, closed-toed shoes for hiking the rocky trails. Arrive early for splash park sessions as they are first-come, first-served with a 75-guest limit. The park is a popular spot for viewing the sunset and the Tulsa skyline from the overlooks Tulsa County Parks TulsaKids Magazine.
attractions.nearby: The Cave House, Gilcrease Museum, Woody Guthrie Center, Philbrook Museum of Art, BOK Center, Arkansas River Tripadvisor

attractions.name: Circle Cinema
attractions.description: Circle Cinema is Tulsa’s longest-standing nonprofit movie theater and the city's last remaining historic movie house. Originally opened in 1928, it promotes community consciousness through film by offering a diverse selection of independent, documentary, foreign, and classic films. (Circle Cinema, Keep It Local OK)
attractions.hours: Monday - Sunday: Opens 30 minutes before the first scheduled showtime each day (typically around 11:00 AM - 11:00 PM) (Circle Cinema, Tripadvisor)
attractions.admission: Member: $8; Non-member Matinee (Mon-Fri before 4pm): $9; Non-member Evening/Weekend: $11; Students, Seniors, Military, Teachers: $9; Student Tuesdays: $2 with valid ID; Special Screenings: typically $11 for members and $13 for non-members (Circle Cinema, Visit Tulsa)
highlights: Listed on the National Register of Historic Places (National Park Service)
Features a functioning 1928 Robert Morton pipe organ used for silent film accompaniment (Circle Cinema)
Home to the Walk of Fame honoring notable Oklahomans in the entertainment industry (Circle Cinema)
On-site art gallery showcasing works by local artists (Circle Cinema)
Unique programming including 2nd Saturday Silents, Noir Nights, and the Circle Cinema Film Festival (Circle Cinema)
attractions.tips: Free parking is available in the lot behind the theater or across Lewis Ave in Whittier Square. Arrive early to browse the rotating art gallery and extensive collection of vintage movie posters in the lobby. (Circle Cinema)
attractions.nearby: Whittier Square Shopping District, Admiral Boulevard (Historic Route 66), Calaveras Mexican Grill, University of Tulsa (National Park Service, Yelp)

attractions.name: Creek Nation Council Oak Park
attractions.description: Creek Nation Council Oak Park is a historic site designating the location where the Lochapoka clan of the Muscogee (Creek) Nation established their new home in 1836 after the Trail of Tears. The park is centered around an ancient post oak tree that served as the tribe's first ceremonial ground and council site, marking the symbolic founding of the city of Tulsa. Today, it remains a sacred landmark and community space dedicated to preserving the rich heritage of the Muscogee people. (Muscogee Nation, Oklahoma Senate)
attractions.region: Riverview Neighborhood (Tallasi Creek Council Oak Tree Foundation)
attractions.hours: 7:00 AM - 10:00 PM (Evendo)
attractions.admission: Free (Visit Tulsa, Evendo)
highlights: The historic Council Oak Tree (Quercus stellata), a living monument to the founding of Tulsa (Gathering Growth)
The 'Morning Prayer' sculpture by Creek artist Dan Brook, honoring the tribe's journey and resilience (Yelp)
An ethno-botanical garden featuring plants historically used by the Creek Nation for medicinal and ceremonial purposes (City of Tulsa)
Interpretive signage detailing the history of the Lochapoka clan and the Trail of Tears (Visit Tulsa)
Annual Creek Nation Council Oak Tree Day ceremonies held every October (MVSKOKE Media)
attractions.tips: The park is a small, quiet urban green space best suited for historical reflection rather than active recreation. Visitors are encouraged to read the interpretive plaques to fully appreciate the site's significance as 'Tulsa's First City Hall.' (Yelp, City of Tulsa)
attractions.nearby: Dream Keepers Park, The Gathering Place, River Parks, Cyrus Avery Centennial Plaza (Evendo, Instagram)

attractions.name: Cyrus Avery Centennial Plaza
attractions.description: A commemorative riverside plaza paying tribute to Cyrus Avery, the 'Father of Route 66,' who was instrumental in routing the highway through Tulsa. The site features detailed historical plaques and a massive bronze sculpture depicting the symbolic transition from animal-powered travel to the age of the automobile. Visit Tulsa, Route 66 Travel Info
attractions.hours: Open 24 hours daily Route 66 Travel Info
attractions.admission: Free Route 66 Travel Info
highlights: 'East Meets West' bronze sculpture depicting the Avery family in a Model T Vision 2025
The historic Cyrus Avery Route 66 Memorial Bridge (former 11th Street Bridge) Wikipedia
Route 66 Skywalk featuring Art Deco design and a large neon shield Route 66 Travel Info
A display of flags from all eight states traversed by Route 66 Vision 2025
Informational kiosks providing a deep dive into Route 66's origins Route 66 Travel Info
attractions.tips: Park in the free lot at the top of the hill near 13th Street and use the Skywalk for panoramic views of the river and bridge. Visit at night to see the neon Route 66 shield illuminated. Route 66 Travel Info
attractions.nearby: The Cave House, Tulsa Spotlight Theater, BOK Center, Center of the Universe, Buck Atom's Cosmic Curios Tripadvisor, Route 66 Travel Info

attractions.name: Grand Lake O' the Cherokees
attractions.description: Grand Lake O' the Cherokees is a sprawling 46,500-acre reservoir nestled in the foothills of the Ozark Mountains in Northeast Oklahoma. Known as one of the state's 'Big Three' lakes, it offers 1,300 miles of shoreline and is a premier destination for world-class bass fishing, sailing, and diverse water sports. The lake was created in 1940 with the completion of the Pensacola Dam, the longest multi-arch dam in the world.
attractions.hours: Lake: Open 24/7; Grand Lake Association: Mon-Fri 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Pensacola Dam Tours: Memorial Day to Labor Day 9:00 AM - 4:00 PM
attractions.admission: Free (Lake access and Pensacola Dam tours are free; local attractions may have individual fees)
highlights: World-class bass fishing with over 400 tournaments annually
Free guided tours of the historic Pensacola Dam during summer months
Expansive 1,300-mile shoreline perfect for boating and sailing
Home to the popular Monkey Island recreation hub and Shangri-La Resort
Host of the annual Pelican Festival celebrating migratory white pelicans
Gateway to the Har-Ber Village Museum, a pioneer-era outdoor museum
attractions.tips: The lake has predictable winds making it a top choice for sailing enthusiasts. Be sure to check the boating rules for high-traffic areas like Duck Creek, which are strictly enforced by the GRDA Lake Patrol. If visiting in the summer, call ahead to schedule a free tour of the Pensacola Dam as walk-in tours may be limited.
attractions.nearby: Bernice State Park, Lendonwood Gardens, Har-Ber Village Museum, Cherokee Casino Grove, Monkey Island, Sailboat Bridge

attractions.name: Hard Rock Hotel & Casino Tulsa (owned and operated by Cherokee Nation Entertainment) (Official Site), (Visit Cherokee Nation)
attractions.description: Hard Rock Hotel & Casino Tulsa is a premier resort destination in Oklahoma, offering a massive gaming floor with over 2,300 electronic games and 50 table games. Managed by Cherokee Nation Entertainment, it features a luxury AAA Four Diamond-awarded hotel, award-winning dining options like Carvers on 19, and a world-class live music venue, Hard Rock Live. (Official Site), (Tripadvisor)

highlights: Over 2,300 electronic games and 50+ table games including poker and blackjack (Official Site)
Hard Rock Live venue hosting top-shelf acts and international tours (Official Site)
AAA Four Diamond-awarded hotel with 454 rooms and suites fit for a rock star (Tripadvisor)

Cherokee Hills Golf Club, an 18-hole historic golf course (Official Site)
One Star® Rewards program offering points, discounts, and exclusive VIP perks (Official Site)
attractions.tips: Join the One Star® Rewards program for free to earn points on slot and poker play immediately. Download the mobile app to track your rewards status and receive the latest promotion updates. Book tickets for Hard Rock Live in advance as popular headliner shows tend to sell out quickly. (Official Site), (Official Site)

attractions.name: Heirloom Rustic Ales
attractions.description: Heirloom Rustic Ales is a neighborhood-focused brewery and taproom that specializes in small-batch beers which move slowly to completion. The brewery emphasizes a simple approach with refreshing, approachable, and affordable offerings, including barrel-aged blended beers, saisons, and lagers. (Heirloom Rustic Ales, TulsaGo)
attractions.hours: Monday: 3-9 PM, Tuesday: 3-9 PM, Wednesday: 3-9 PM, Thursday: 3-9 PM, Friday: 3-10 PM, Saturday: 12-10 PM, Sunday: 11-5 PM (Heirloom Rustic Ales)
attractions.admission: Free (Heirloom Rustic Ales)
highlights: Brewing small-batch, slow-moving beers (Heirloom Rustic Ales)
Focus on barrel-aged blended beers, saisons, and lagers (The Humble Garnish)
Taproom centerpiece bar made with reclaimed Oklahoma lumber (The Humble Garnish)
Hosts local food trucks like Old Dog Thai Food Truck (Heirloom Rustic Ales)
Community-oriented events like plant swaps and trivia nights (Heirloom Rustic Ales)
Extensive tap list featuring unique options like Yuzu Lager and Petite French Lager (Heirloom Rustic Ales)
attractions.tips: The taproom is pet-friendly (dogs allowed on leash) and all-ages. While they don't have a kitchen, local food trucks are often on-site Friday through Sunday, and outside food is welcome. Non-alcoholic options like house-brewed NA beer, locally made sodas, and juice boxes are also available. (Heirloom Rustic Ales)

attractions.name: Inner Circle Vodka Bar
attractions.description: Inner Circle Vodka Bar is a premier nightlife destination in the Tulsa Arts District specializing in house-infused vodka cocktails. The establishment features a spacious outdoor patio equipped with giant games, live music, and unique seasonal amenities like heated winter globes. Official Website, Visit Tulsa

Large outdoor patio area with interactive giant games like Jenga, cornhole, and trash can pong.
Seasonal interactive events including cocktail infusion classes and themed holiday parties.
Heated outdoor winter globes available for private group rentals during colder months.
Dog-friendly atmosphere with treats available for four-legged guests on the patio.
Regular live music performances and high-energy weekend DJ sets.
attractions.tips: Arrive early on weekends to secure a spot before the post-concert crowds from Cain's Ballroom arrive. Reserve winter globes or summer cabanas online in advance to guarantee outdoor seating. Check the official calendar for infusion classes if you want a more structured experience. Official Website, Tripadvisor

attractions.name: Juniper Restaurant
attractions.description: Juniper is an award-winning farm-to-table restaurant in downtown Tulsa that specializes in seasonal, creative, and modern cuisine using locally sourced ingredients. Designed by Chef Justin Thompson, the space offers an intimate atmosphere featuring rustic brick columns and commissioned local artwork. Guests can enjoy a rotating a la carte menu, a signature five-course tasting menu, and unique craft cocktails made with house-infused spirits (Juniper, Tripadvisor).
attractions.hours: Monday: 4:00 PM - 9:00 PM, Tuesday: 4:00 PM - 9:00 PM, Wednesday: 4:00 PM - 9:00 PM, Thursday: 4:00 PM - 9:00 PM, Friday: 4:00 PM - 10:00 PM, Saturday: 4:00 PM - 10:00 PM, Sunday: Closed (Juniper)

Rotating seasonal menus featuring locally sourced ingredients (Juniper)
Signature five-course Chef’s tasting menu with optional wine pairings (Juniper)
Extensive craft cocktail program utilizing house-infused spirits (Tripadvisor)
Intimate downtown setting with rustic brick columns and local art (Tripadvisor)
attractions.tips: Reservations are highly recommended due to the restaurant's intimate size and popularity for special occasions. Don't miss the signature Whipped Goat Cheese appetizer or the Beef Stroganoff. A special bar menu with smaller plates is available during Happy Hour on weekdays from 4:00 PM to 6:00 PM. Note that downtown parking can be challenging, especially on weekends (Wanderlog, Juniper, Tripadvisor).
attractions.nearby: Blue Dome Station, Dust Bowl Lanes & Lounge, Arnie's Bar, The Boxyard, Bob Dylan Center (Tripadvisor, Evendo)

attractions.name: Keystone State Park
attractions.description: Keystone State Park is located in northeast Oklahoma on the shores of the scenic 26,000-acre Keystone Lake, just a short drive west of Tulsa. The park serves as a premier destination for water sports, fishing, and hiking, featuring diverse landscapes from rocky shorelines to wooded hills. Visitors can enjoy extensive amenities including fully furnished cabins, multiple campgrounds, a full-service marina, and family-friendly facilities like a children's fishing pond. (TravelOK)
attractions.hours: Park Office: 9:00 am - 6:00 pm Daily; Park remains open year-round for visitors and campers. (TravelOK)
attractions.admission: Daily Parking: $8.00 (In-state), $10.00 (Out-of-state). Annual Pass: $60.00 (In-state), $75.00 (Out-of-state). Free for Oklahoma residents 62+ and honorably discharged Oklahoma veterans. 50% discount for disabled individuals. (Oklahoma State Parks_0.pdf))
highlights: Access to Keystone Lake for boating, water skiing, and professional-grade fishing. (TravelOK)
22 furnished cabins with kitchens, satellite TV, fireplaces, and select Jacuzzi tubs. (TravelOK)
Pier 51 Marina, which offers boat rentals, fuel, groceries, and a floating restaurant. (TravelOK)
Three distinct campgrounds (Red Carpet, Green Country, and Lake Country) with full RV hookups and tent sites. (TravelOK)
On-site children's fishing pond and multiple playgrounds for family activities. (TravelOK)
Proximity to off-road vehicle (ATV/ORV) areas located below the Keystone Dam. (TravelOK)
attractions.tips: Parking passes are required for all vehicles and can be purchased online or via the mobile app to avoid delays. Oklahoma residents aged 62 and older or honorably discharged veterans can apply for a free lifetime parking pass. It is highly recommended to book cabins and campsites well in advance through the online reservation portal. (Reddit/Tulsa, TravelOK)
attractions.nearby: Pier 51 Marina, Keystone Ancient Forest, Jellystone Park™ Keystone Lake, Sand Springs, Tulsa (15 miles east). (TravelOK)

attractions.name: Marshall Brewing Company
attractions.description: Marshall Brewing Company is Tulsa's original production craft brewery, founded in 2008 to bring the art and quality of craft brewing to Oklahoma. Led by German-trained Brewmaster Eric Marshall, the brewery focuses on hand-crafting full-strength ales and lagers using traditional techniques and high-quality raw materials Official Website.
attractions.hours: Monday: 3:00 PM - 8:00 PM, Tuesday: 3:00 PM - 8:00 PM, Wednesday: 11:00 AM - 9:00 PM, Thursday: 11:00 AM - 9:00 PM, Friday: 11:00 AM - 10:00 PM, Saturday: 12:00 PM - 10:00 PM, Sunday: 1:00 PM - 7:00 PM Official Website
attractions.days_open: Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday Official Website
attractions.admission: Free to visit the Tap Room; Private Tours (15-30 people) require a $50 facility fee plus $3/person/brand Official Website
highlights: Tulsa's first production craft brewery since World War II Apple Maps
Tap Room features 22 taps, including LUKR side pulls and cask beer engines Official Website
Spacious outdoor Biergarten and family/dog-friendly atmosphere Official Website
Founded by a Brewmaster trained at the prestigious World Brewing Academy in Munich Official Website
Offers a wide range of core and seasonal beers, including German-style pilsners and English bitters Apple Maps
attractions.tips: The Tap Room is family and dog-friendly and allows visitors to have food delivered from local restaurants Oklahoma Agritourism. Visit during the week for a quieter experience or on weekends for limited-production selections Marriott Bonvoy Traveler.

attractions.name: Mary K. Oxley Nature Center
attractions.description: The Mary K. Oxley Nature Center is an 800-acre wildlife sanctuary located within Tulsa's Mohawk Park, offering over nine miles of trails through diverse habitats like forests, wetlands, and prairies. It serves as a premier destination for birding and nature exploration, featuring an interpretive center with hands-on exhibits and educational programs (Oxley Nature Center About, Lonely Planet).
attractions.hours: Interpretive Building: Tue-Sat 10:00 AM - 4:30 PM, Sun 12:00 PM - 4:30 PM (Closed Mon); Hiking Trails: Daily 7:00 AM - 9:00 PM; Gate/Parking: Daily 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM (Oxley Nature Center)
attractions.days_open: Tuesday–Sunday (Interpretive Building); Daily (Trails and Parking) (Oxley Nature Center)
attractions.admission: Free admission; Guided school field trips are $2 per student (Oxley Nature Center, Group Tours)
highlights: Over nine miles of hiking trails through diverse ecosystems (Oxley Nature Center)
Oxley-Yetter Interpretive Building with hands-on exhibits (Oxley Nature Center Attractions)
Over 260 documented species of birds, making it a birder's paradise (Oxley Nature Center Tips)
Blackbird Marsh with a 600-foot boardwalk for wetland study (Oxley Nature Center Attractions)
70-acre hand-dug lake built by the CCC during the Great Depression (Oxley Nature Center About)
Wildlife observation tower overlooking Blackbird Marsh (Oxley Nature Center Attractions)
attractions.tips: No pets, bikes, or motorized vehicles are allowed on trails to protect the wildlife habitat. Visitors should bring their own water and sun protection as there are no vending machines on site (Oxley Nature Center Tips).
attractions.nearby: Tulsa Zoo, Mohawk Park, Tulsa Air and Space Museum & Planetarium, Lake Yahola (Tripadvisor, Wikipedia)

attractions.name: Minnie Ha Ha Park
attractions.description: Minnie Ha Ha Park is a scenic 49.7-acre destination situated along the Meramec River, offering a mix of natural beauty and recreational facilities. Opened in 2005 on the site of the former 1930s Minnie Ha Ha Beach, the park features lush woodlands, open fields, and picturesque river overlooks. (Sunset Hills Official Site, Pacer)
attractions.address: 801 Old Gravois Rd, St. Louis, MO 63127 (Sunset Hills Official Site)
attractions.city: St. Louis (Sunset Hills) (Sunset Hills Official Site)
attractions.region: Sunset Hills / South St. Louis County (Sunset Hills Official Site)
attractions.hours: 7:00 AM – 9:00 PM daily (Sunset Hills Official Site)
attractions.admission: Free (Sunset Hills Official Site)
highlights: 3.4 miles of paved walking and hiking trails (Thataway Dad)
Scenic overlooks with views of the Meramec River (Sunset Hills Official Site)
Three distinct playground areas including a pirate ship and zipline (Thataway Dad)
Professional athletic facilities including two soccer fields and a disc golf course (Sunset Hills Official Site)
Public fishing access and river viewing spots (Sunset Hills Official Site)
Historical plaques detailing the park's history as a ferry site and beach (Thataway Dad)
attractions.tips: Visit during the spring or fall for the best scenery and mild weather. Be mindful that sections of the lower trails near the river are prone to flooding after heavy rains. The park is 'cupless' for certain endurance events, so bring your own water bottle for long stays. (Pacer, St. Louis Ultrarunners Group)
attractions.nearby: Fenton City Park, George Winter Park, Tapawingo Golf Club, and Laumeier Sculpture Park (Pacer, Thataway Dad)

attractions.name: Mohawk Park
attractions.description: Mohawk Park is a sprawling 2,800-acre destination in Tulsa, Oklahoma, serving as the largest park in the city's system since its establishment in 1924 (City of Tulsa). It offers a diverse range of recreational opportunities, including the world-class Tulsa Zoo and the serene Mary K. Oxley Nature Center (Visit Tulsa). Visitors can enjoy extensive hiking trails, picnic areas, fishing ponds, and a 36-hole golf course within its scenic, natural landscape (Oklahoma Department of Wildlife Conservation).
attractions.region: Mohawk Park-Dawson / North Tulsa (Tulsa Planning Office)
attractions.hours: Daily: 7:00 AM – 9:00 PM (Oxley Nature Center). Specific facility hours vary: Oxley Interpretive Building is typically open Mon-Sat 10:00 AM – 4:30 PM and Sun 12:00 PM – 4:30 PM (closed Fridays) (Oxley Nature Center).
attractions.days_open: Open daily (7 days a week) (Oxley Nature Center).
attractions.admission: General admission is free. A $2 parking fee per vehicle and $10 per bus applies on weekends and holidays from April 1 to October 31 (Tulsa Zoo, TulsaKids Magazine). Specific attractions like the Tulsa Zoo and Mohawk Park Golf Course have separate fees (Tulsa Golf).
highlights: Tulsa Zoo: An 85-acre home to animals from around the world (Tripadvisor).
Mary K. Oxley Nature Center: Over 800 acres of trails and wildlife habitats (Oxley Nature Center).
Mohawk Park Golf Course: Features 36 holes of public golf (Tulsa Golf).
Mohawk Sports Complex: A multi-use facility for soccer and other sports (City of Tulsa).
Picnic Areas & Playgrounds: Extensive space for family gatherings and water playgrounds (Visit Tulsa).
Fishing: Close-to-Home fishing managed at park ponds (Oklahoma Department of Wildlife Conservation).
attractions.tips: The park and golf facilities are cashless, so plan accordingly (Tulsa Golf). If arriving early for hiking, you may park outside the gates to access trails between 7:00 AM and 8:00 AM (Oxley Nature Center). A small parking fee is required on weekends and holidays during the peak season (April-October) (Tulsa Zoo).
attractions.nearby: Tulsa International Airport, Admiral Twin Drive-In, Route 66 attractions (City of Tulsa).

attractions.name: Oklahoma Aquarium Oklahoma Aquarium
attractions.description: The Oklahoma Aquarium is a 72,000-square-foot public aquarium featuring over 500 species and 10,000 animals. It is home to the world's largest exhibit of bull sharks and offers a unique walk-through tunnel for visitors to explore. The facility also features exhibits focusing on Oklahoma species, Ozark habitats, and coral reefs. Wikipedia
attractions.hours: Mon, Wed-Sun: 10 a.m. – 6 p.m.; Tue: 10 a.m. – 9 p.m. Oklahoma Aquarium
attractions.days_open: Open daily year-round (except Thanksgiving and Christmas Day) Oklahoma Aquarium

highlights: Shark Adventure: Walk-through tunnel with the world's largest collection of bull sharks Oklahoma Aquarium
Sea Turtle Island: Home to a 300-pound loggerhead sea turtle Oklahoma Aquarium
Polynesian Reef: Vibrant tropical fish and coral Oklahoma Aquarium
Aquatic Oklahoma: Native freshwater species like alligator snapping turtles and paddlefish Oklahoma Aquarium
Extreme Amazon: A 3,800-gallon freshwater tunnel with piranhas and stingrays Oklahoma Aquarium
EcoZone: Features touch tanks and diverse habitats from rocky coasts to marshes Oklahoma Aquarium
attractions.tips: Last admission is one hour before closing; Tuesdays offer extended evening hours until 9 p.m.; checking the daily feed schedule is recommended to see animals in action. Oklahoma Aquarium
attractions.nearby: Gathering Place, BOK Center, Philbrook Museum of Art, Tulsa Zoo, and River Spirit Casino Oklahoma Aquarium

attractions.name: Oologah Lake
attractions.description: Oologah Lake is a prominent reservoir in northeastern Oklahoma, managed by the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers for flood control, water supply, and recreation. Spanning over 29,000 acres of surface water, it is widely recognized as one of the best lakes in the region for sailing due to its wide stretches and consistent winds (Wikipedia). Visitors can enjoy a variety of outdoor activities including fishing for world-record-sized catfish, camping in eleven developed parks, and exploring miles of multi-use trails (Green Country Oklahoma).
attractions.hours: Project Office: Monday - Friday, 8:00 AM - 4:30 PM; Recreation Areas: Open daily for day use and camping (U.S. Army Corps of Engineers)
attractions.admission: Day Use: $5 per private vehicle or $2 per person (walk-in/bike-in); Camping: $16 - $50 per night; Children under 16: Free; Annual Day Use Pass: $40 (U.S. Army Corps of Engineers)
highlights: Renowned for excellent sailing conditions on wide-open waters (U.S. Army Corps of Engineers)
Home to 11 public use areas offering diverse camping and picnicking facilities (Recreation.gov)
Exceptional fishing for blue catfish, sand bass, walleye, and hybrid striped bass (Oklahoma Wildlife Department)
18-mile Will Rogers Country Centennial Trail for hiking and horseback riding (Wikipedia)
Abundant wildlife and bird-watching opportunities, including bald eagles (Green Country Oklahoma)
Relocated birthplace ranch of famed humorist Will Rogers located on its shores (Visit Cherokee Nation)
attractions.tips: Visitors are encouraged to download the Recreation.gov mobile app for convenient payment of day-use fees and making camping reservations. For a calmer experience with paddleboarding or kayaking, the sheltered waters of Blue Creek Park are highly recommended (Reddit). Always check the latest lake levels and water safety advisories on the official USACE site before arriving.
attractions.nearby: Will Rogers Birthplace Ranch, Will Rogers Memorial & Museum, J.M. Davis Arms & Historical Museum, Redbud Marina, and Claremore Lake

attractions.name: Route 66 Historical Village
attractions.description: The Route 66 Historical Village is an open-air museum and transportation theme park that preserves Tulsa’s history in the oil, refining, and transportation industries. Visitors can explore a collection of historic artifacts, including a restored 1920s-style gas station and massive vintage machinery. (Official Site) (Visit Tulsa)
attractions.region: Southwest Tulsa / Red Fork District (Official Site) (Visit Tulsa)
attractions.hours: Outdoor Museum: Open 24/7 year-round. Visitor Center (April–Nov): Friday–Sunday 12:00 PM – 3:00 PM; Closed Dec–March. (Official Site) (Tripadvisor)
attractions.days_open: Outdoor area: Daily; Visitor Center: Friday–Sunday (April–November) (Official Site) (Tripadvisor)
attractions.admission: Free (Official Site) (Tripadvisor)
highlights: Frisco 4500 Meteor Steam Engine (Official Site)
154-foot tall Centennial Derrick, the tallest in North America (Official Site)
Replica 1920s Phillips 66 cottage-style gas station (Official Site)
Restored Pullman passenger car and vintage caboose (Official Site)
Oil field pump jacks and vintage rail cars (Official Site)
Route 66 Highway Shield for photo opportunities (Official Site)
attractions.tips: Visit during the evening to see the Centennial Derrick and nearby neon signs illuminated. The Visitor Center is only open on weekends from April to November, so plan accordingly if you want to see the interior exhibits. (Adventures of A+K) (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Sand Springs Cultural & Historical Museum
attractions.description: The Sand Springs Cultural & Historical Museum is housed in the historic Page Memorial Library building, a stunning example of Art Deco architecture listed on the National Register of Historic Places. It preserves and highlights the local heritage of Oklahoma's only planned industrial town, including the story of founder Charles Page and his benevolent legacy. (Official Website, Sandite Pride News)

highlights: Historic Page Memorial Library building, a 1929 Art Deco landmark (Official Website)
Permanent exhibits on city founder Charles Page, the Sand Springs Home, and Widow's Colony (Official Website)
Unique architectural features including Tennessee pink marble and original cork floors (Tripadvisor)
Genealogical research area located in the museum basement (Tripadvisor)
Rotating traveling exhibits and local art gallery displays (Official Website)

attractions.name: Sequoyah State Park
attractions.description: Sequoyah State Park is a 2,200-acre peninsular recreation area situated on the shores of Fort Gibson Lake in northeast Oklahoma. The park is a premier destination for water sports, fishing, and hiking, featuring 225 miles of shoreline and over 12 miles of nature trails. It also houses the state's largest lodge, a nine-hole golf course, and the Three Forks Nature Center. TravelOK, Wikipedia
attractions.hours: Park: Open 24 hours daily. Three Forks Nature Center: Tuesday – Sunday, 9:00 AM – 4:00 PM. Park Office, Nature Center, and Golf Course are closed on Christmas Day. TravelOK
attractions.days_open: Daily (365 days a year); Three Forks Nature Center is open Tuesday – Sunday. TravelOK
attractions.admission: Parking fees: $10.00/day for out-of-state vehicles, $8.00/day for vehicles with Oklahoma or tribal license plates. Discounts: 100% off for Oklahoma residents age 62+ and their spouses, 100% off for Oklahoma resident veterans, and 50% off for individuals certified as totally disabled. Oklahoma State Senate_0.pdf), Wikipedia
highlights: Fort Gibson Lake: 19,000 surface acres of water for boating, fishing, and swimming. TravelOK
Three Forks Nature Center: Features interpretive displays and an ambassador animal program. TravelOK
Sequoyah State Park Golf Course: A 9-hole course with Ultradwarf Bermuda grass greens and lake views. TravelOK
Sequoyah Riding Stables: Offers guided trail rides, hayrides, and a Cowboy Camp. TravelOK
Paradise Cove Marina: A full-service marina with boat rentals, sales, and supplies. TravelOK
12 miles of hiking trails: Includes the Fossil Trail and the Bluebird Trail for wildlife watching. Wikipedia
attractions.tips: Visitors are encouraged to purchase a parking pass in advance online or via the mobile app. The zero-entry swimming pool is restricted to lodge and cabin guests, while a lakeside swim beach is available for all visitors. Firewood should be purchased from on-site automated kiosks to protect the forest from invasive species. TravelOK
attractions.nearby: Fort Gibson Historic Site, Longshadows Ranch, Tahlequah, and Sequoyah Bay State Park. Tripadvisor, Tahlequah Area Chamber of Commerce

attractions.name: Skiatook Lake USACE
attractions.category: Park, Reservoir, Recreation Area Green Country Oklahoma
attractions.description: Skiatook Lake is a beautiful man-made reservoir located on Hominy Creek in Osage County, Oklahoma. The lake is surrounded by gentle rolling hills of Blackjack and Post Oak, interspersed with Tallgrass Prairie. It is well-known for its crystal-clear waters and steep picturesque bluffs that offer spectacular views for recreation users. Visitors can enjoy a wide range of outdoor activities including boating, hiking, swimming, camping, and high-quality fishing. USACE
attractions.region: Osage County / Green Country Oklahoma Green Country Oklahoma
attractions.hours: Lake areas are generally accessible 24/7; developed park gates close from 10:00 PM to 6:00 AM daily. The Lake Office is open Monday-Friday 8:30 AM - 5:00 PM and Saturday 10:00 AM - 4:00 PM. Recreation.gov, Skiatook Lake Online Guide
attractions.days_open: Open daily year-round; however, specific parks like Tall Chief Cove and Twin Points operate seasonally (typically opening April 1st). Skiatook Lake Online Guide
attractions.admission: General access is free; Day-use fees at designated parks are $5 per private vehicle, $2 per person for walk-in/bike-in, and $20 for buses/commercial vehicles. Children under 16 are free. USACE
highlights: Crystal-clear waters ideal for swimming, boating, and water sports Airial Travel
Steep picturesque bluffs providing scenic views and unique shoreline topography Green Country Oklahoma
Premier fishing destination for hybrid striped bass, crappie, largemouth bass, and catfish Green Country Oklahoma
Developed recreation areas such as Tall Chief Cove and Twin Points for camping and picnicking Recreation.gov
Home to the full-service Cross Timbers Marina and luxury lakeside cottages Green Country Oklahoma
Scenic hiking and nature trails set within the diverse Oklahoma landscape USACE
attractions.tips: Visitors are strongly encouraged to wear life jackets at all times for water safety. The restaurant also serves local Hawaiian favorites, including burgers made with grass-fed beef and traditional plate lunches. (Official Website, Gayot)

attractions.name: The University of Tulsa
attractions.description: The University of Tulsa (TU) is a private research university located in Tulsa, Oklahoma, known for its strong programs in petroleum engineering and its historic affiliation with the Presbyterian Church. The campus is characterized by its Collegiate Gothic architecture and centers around a wide grassy quad known as Dietler Commons (Wikipedia).
attractions.hours: Campus Window Hours: Monday – Friday, 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM; Tours: Mon-Fri (and Sat from Labor Day to May 1) (University of Tulsa, University of Tulsa Visit)

Historic McFarlin Library housing over 12 million archival items and the Bob Dylan Archive (Wikipedia).
Collegiate Gothic campus architecture featuring tan and rose-colored sandstone buildings (Wikipedia).
Home of the Tulsa Golden Hurricane, competing in NCAA Division I athletics (Wikipedia).
Management of the Gilcrease Museum, which holds one of the world's largest collections of American Western art (Wikipedia).
attractions.tips: Prospective students should schedule tours in advance to meet with faculty; visitor parking is available in designated lots but requires attention to regulations to avoid fines (University of Tulsa, University of Tulsa Parking).

attractions.description: The Gilcrease Museum houses the world's most comprehensive collection of art and artifacts of the American West, representing over 12,000 years of history. Set within 460 acres of the scenic Osage Hills, it features more than 350,000 items and 11 themed gardens reflecting various historical periods Gilcrease Museum, Wikipedia.

attractions.admission: Free for UNcrease events (registration required) and for children under 18; Regular admission (reopening 2027): $8 adults, $6 seniors (62+), $5 college students, Free for TU students and members Gilcrease Museum

More than 350,000 items representing hundreds of Indigenous cultures across the Americas
460 acres of grounds featuring 11 themed gardens such as Pre-Columbian, Pioneer, and Victorian styles
Helmerich Center for American Research, housing over 100,000 rare books and manuscripts
Newly redesigned 84,000-square-foot state-of-the-art facility opening early 2027

attractions.name: Tulsa Air and Space Museum & Planetarium
attractions.description: The Tulsa Air and Space Museum (TASM) is a premier aerospace heritage site in Tulsa, Oklahoma, featuring a vast collection of restored vintage aircraft and interactive exhibits. Visitors can explore the history of Oklahoma’s aviation industry, walk through a full-size MD-80 jet, and experience immersive astronomical shows at the on-site James E. Bertelsmeyer Planetarium. The museum's mission is to honor the past while inspiring the future through hands-on science-based education. Tulsa Air & Space Museum
attractions.region: Tulsa International Airport / Mohawk Park area
attractions.hours: Monday - Saturday: 10:00 am – 4:00 pm, Sunday: Closed
attractions.admission: Museum Only: Adult $11, Senior (62+) $9, Military $9, Youth (5-12) $8, Kids (0-4) Free. Combo (Museum + 1 Planetarium Show): Adult $20, Senior $16, Military $16, Youth $14, Kids Free. Planetarium Only: Adult $11, Senior/Military $9, Youth $8, Kids Free. Discounts available through Museums for All program ($3 combo tickets with valid ID). Official Tickets
highlights: Grumman F-14A Tomcat fighter jet cockpit experience
Full-size American Airlines MD-80 aircraft available for tours
James E. Bertelsmeyer Planetarium with immersive 360° shows
Replica of Tulsa's original Art Deco airport terminal from the 1930s
Vintage aircraft collection including the Spartan Executive and Rockwell Ranger 2000
Interactive 'Mission Control' shuttle launch simulator
attractions.tips: The museum does not have an on-site restaurant, but guests can bring sack lunches to enjoy at the pavilion or purchase snacks at the gift shop. Plan for 2-3 hours to fully explore the museum and see a planetarium show. All main areas, including the MD-80 jet, are wheelchair and stroller accessible. Official Website FAQ

attractions.name: Tulsa Botanic Garden
attractions.description: The Tulsa Botanic Garden is a 170-acre all-season oasis blooming in the Osage Hills, featuring a dynamic collection of plants, art, and architecture. It includes major attractions like the 3-acre Tandy Floral Terraces and the Driskill Children's Discovery Garden, offering a place for inspiration, reflection, and discovery. Official Site, Wikipedia

attractions.admission: Members: FREE, Adults (Age 16+): $10, Youth (Age 3-15): $5, Children 2 & under: Free (ticket required). Discounted $5 admission for reciprocal members and special pricing for SNAP/WIC/Foster Parent card holders. Official Site
highlights: A.R. and Marylouise Tandy Floral Terraces: A 3-acre ornamental garden with over 8,000 plants and water features. Official Site

Located in the Tulsa Arts District, it serves as a vital resource for scholars and fans, housing the world's largest collection of Guthrie's primary source materials. Visitors can explore displays on the Dust Bowl, his social activism, and his enduring influence on American history and culture (Official Website).

Interactive 'Woody's America' touch screen map exploring his life and world history (Our Changing Life)
